prevalence.trend: Increasing by 1-2% annually due to aging population and lifestyle changes

incidence.source: WHO Global Burden of Disease Study 2021, adapted for India

riskFactors: Occupational factors (prolonged squatting, heavy manual labor in agriculture)
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical symptoms (pain, stiffness) + radiographic evidence (Kellgren-Lawrence grade ≥2); ACR/EULAR criteria adapted for resource-limited settings

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate (40-50% symptom management in urban areas; lower in rural)

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Similar to global standards but emphasis on clinical diagnosis due to limited imaging access; squatting test for knee OA
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Conservative: NSAIDs, physiotherapy, weight management; Surgical: Joint replacement in advanced cases; Integration of Ayurveda (e.g., Panchakarma) in some regions
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High prevalence due to cultural squatting habits and occupational demands in rural India; Limited access to orthopedics in remote areas; Overburdened public hospitals
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Growing awareness via national programs like NPCDCS; Affordable generic medications; Community-based rehabilitation in some states
